what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard background note that frames an interface-architecture problem: the operator interface grew through fast direct edits into a monolithic file, causing drift and duplication, and it deliberately stops at stating problems and principles rather than proposing a plan.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board address, type block, source claude_code, state current, title, author_session, channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-b1f59e67.
History shows the item was filed and later edited to reframe it as background/problems/principles with no plan.
Body explicitly labels itself as NOT a plan, NOT proposals, and NOT specifics on how to do it.
Identifies the current operator interface as a ~750-line monolith with HTML/CSS/JS inline.
Describes the crumbling pattern: fast direct edits → un-typed special cases → no single source of truth → duplication → drift between code and docs → collapse.
Calls the current interface working scaffolding that proved features but is not the architecture.
States the 'how' will be resolved through Tim's principles and architecture, learned by the fork and its own research.
standouts: The document's main purpose is to NOT prescribe a solution, explicitly to avoid biasing the build.
It reframes the existing working UI as disposable scaffolding rather than a foundation.
It names a specific anti-pattern: fast direct edits to one growing file leading to collapse.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter, id, address, type, source, state, links, and history.
Uses a board:// URI for cross-item linking.
Markdown body uses a top-level heading and short sections.
Breaks the convention of planning documents by refusing to include a plan or proposals.
concerns: History transition from 'edit' to 'title,body' is non-standard compared to state-to-state transitions.
References 'Tim's principles' and 'the fork' without defining them from this file alone.
The 'no plan' stance leaves all implementation specifics absent, which is intentional but may make the item hard to act on in isolation.
notes: A self-aware background note that diagnoses interface drift from ad-hoc monolith edits and intentionally refuses to propose a solution so the architecture can emerge from Tim's principles.
questions: Who is Tim and what are the specific principles referred to?
What is 'the fork' in this context?
Which file is the ~750-line monolithic operator interface?
What are the next few changes that are expected to trigger the collapse?
How will the fork learn from Tim and from its own research?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is background, problems, and principles — not a plan; the actual content matches that purpose and deliberately avoids prescribing solutions.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a shared problem framing and a boundary for re-architecting the operator interface, ensuring design emerges from Tim's architecture and research rather than from a prematurely written plan.
novelty: A planning-adjacent document whose explicit value is that it refuses to propose a plan, and that it treats the currently working interface as proven scaffolding rather than a stable foundation.
contribution: Provides a concise, shared diagnosis of interface drift and a non-prescriptive constraint that future design work can reference, keeping problem framing separate from solution proposals.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard background note framing how the operator interface became a drifting monolith through fast direct edits, declaring the current UI as proven scaffolding and refusing to prescribe an architectural plan so the build can learn from Tim's principles.
intention_for_embedding: To establish a problem framing and non-prescriptive boundary for re-architecting the operator interface.
